{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01CK6wwgEq2n1LP4QVdfHsPL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01G11hf2mEssXRvin3Et23Gc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs","content":"//! Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 31; // 31 seconds in the past, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 seconds from now, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 6: `jti` revoked → 401 credential_revoked\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jti_revoked() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    // Insert the JTI directly into the revocation cache.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n/// Row 7: `iss` unknown → 401 credential_foreign_issuer\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_foreign_issuer() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.iss = \"some-other-issuer\".to_string();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_foreign_issuer\");\n}\n\n/// Row 8: `aud` ≠ URL tenant → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_wrong_tenant() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.aud = \"other-tenant\".to_string(); // URL says \"acme\"\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_wrong_tenant\");\n}\n\n/// Row 9: `sub` suspended → 401 user_suspended\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_user_suspended() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Build state with a suspended user.\n    let mut storage = make_storage();\n    // Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 31; // 31 seconds in the past, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = URL tenant → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_wrong_tenant() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = \"credential_wrong_tenant\");\n}\n\n/// Row 9: `sub` suspended → 401 user_suspended\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_user_suspended() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    // Grant is for \"products\", URL database is \"orders\"\n    claims.grants = Grants {\n state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    // Second request must fail with credential_revoked.\n    let (status, json) = send(app_after, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n    ];\n\n    // Use a route that accepts all HTTP methods.\n    let handler = || async { StatusCode::OK };\n\n    for case in cases {\n        let issuer_inner = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n        let mut claims = valid_claims();\n        claims.grants = Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n .method(case.method.clone())\n            .uri(TEST_PATH)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n            .body(Body::empty())\n            .unwrap();\n        let // Verify every variant maps to the correct status and code string.\n    let cases: &[(AuthError, u16, &str)] = &[\n        (AuthError::Unauthenticated, 401, \"unauthenticated\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialMalformed, 401, \"credential_malformed\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialInvalid, 401, \"credential_invalid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialExpired, 401, Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":575,"cache_read_input_tokens":132304,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":575},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"cc1c03fa-01b8-4915-97d1-edc4d799b3f6"}

SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n//!\n//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat(auth-pipeline): sign + verify JWT + middleware + revocation [axon-dccbdf73]\n\nImplements the full ADR-018 §4 JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What changed\n\n### axon-core (additive)\n- `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` — installed by middleware\n- `AuthError::status_code()` / `error_code()` — 401/403 + stable code strings\n- `Grants::find_database()` / `GrantedDatabase::has_op()` — grant helpers\n- `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `ResolvedIdentity` re-exported from lib.rs\n\n### axon-storage (additive)\n- `StorageAdapter::is_jti_revoked(Uuid)`, `get_user(UserId)`,\n  `get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId)` with default no-op impls\n- `MemoryStorageAdapter`: stores revoked JTIs, users, tenant_members;\n  test helpers `insert_user`, `insert_tenant_member`, `revoke_jti`\n- SQLite + Postgres: query `credential_revocations`, `users`, `tenant_users`;\n  graceful no-op when auth tables not yet migrated\n\n### axon-server (new)\n- `auth_pipeline.rs`: `JwtIssuer` (HS256 sign/verify), `InMemoryRevocationCache`,\n  `AuthPipelineState`, `jwt_verify_layer` (8-step ADR-018 verification order),\n adds two quality hooks to the layer-2/layer-3 lifecycle. The hooks are
implemented in `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime` at the same boundary that already
owns bead selection, attempt finalization, and retry classification.

`PreClaimIntakeHook` runs after a bead has been selected and verified as
dependency-eligible, but before DDx claims it or creates the implementation
worktree. It receives the bead record, current execution policy, hook mode
(`WARN-ONLY` or `BLOCK`), and the layer-3 evidence directory. It invokes the
bead-lifecycle workflow skill from FEAT-011 and writes an actionability,
scope, and decomposition report. In WARN-ONLY mode, the report is diagnostic
unless the hook can safely improve or decompose the bead. In BLOCK/factory mode,
a valid low actionability score, unsafe ambiguity, or too-large classification
stops implementation before claim.

`PostAttemptTriageHook` runs after the attempt has produced its owned
evidence: commits or no-changes rationale, command results, review verdicts,
merge/preserve result, and any structural validation result. It invokes the
same bead-lifecycle workflow skill against the attempt bundle and returns an
attempt classification such as prompt-quality issue, task-quality issue,
infrastructure failure, deterministic setup failure, or operator action
required. This classification feeds retry reporting and operator UX; it does
not replace the existing success/no-changes/failed outcome taxonomy.

Both hooks are fail-open for infrastructure failures. If the skill package is
missing, the hook process crashes, evidence cannot be written, or the model
invocation fails, DDx records the hook error and continues with the underlying
attempt flow in WARN-ONLY mode. In BLOCK/factory mode, hook infrastructure
failure may skip the current candidate for the pass with observable evidence,
but it must not park the bead behind an unexplained cooldown. Only a
successfully computed intake/lint result can block dispatch as an authoring or
scope failure. `--force --reason <text>` records an event with the actor,
reason, hook mode, and overridden criteria before proceeding.

`ExecuteBeadReport` gains an `OutcomeReason` field beside the existing
`Disrupted` flag. `Disrupted` remains the coarse boolean that the attempt did
not complete normally. `OutcomeReason` is the machine-readable reason selected
by lifecycle classification, for example:

- `bead_lint_warn`
- `bead_lint_blocked`
- `intake_actionable_atomic`
- `intake_rewritten`
- `intake_decomposed`
- `intake_needs_human`
- `forced_with_reason`
- `triage_prompt_quality`
- `triage_task_quality`
- `triage_infrastructure_failure`
- `triage_operator_action`
- `hook_unavailable_fail_open`

Layer-3 `ddx work` records include the hook summaries for each child layer-2
attempt in the stop-condition evaluation log. A blocked lint result counts as a
terminal non-success outcome for that bead under the current policy, so the
existing `blocked`, `deferred`, and `no_progress` loop rules can reason about
it without a fourth run layer or a separate run type.

## No Run-Type Catalog

DDx will not introduce additional run kinds beyond the three layers.
Comparison, replay, benchmark, adversarial review, effort estimate,

- unanimous reviewer `APPROVE` with per-AC evidence permits close;
- any evidenced `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `BLOCK` prevents close;
- reviewer disagreement is preserved in the review group evidence, and the
  non-approve verdict wins when it cites evidence;
- malformed, empty, context-overflow, and transport failures are
  `review-error` classes scoped to the reviewed `result_rev` and reviewer.

Non-approve reviewer findings are classified before the next action:

- `review_fixable_gap` — implementation or test work is missing, incomplete, or
  erroneous. DDx schedules a new implementation cycle on the same bead when
  retry budgets allow. The next implementation prompt includes the review
  findings as required repair context. DDx may raise `MinPower` only when the
  finding is plausibly capability-sensitive.
- `review_spec_gap` / `review_missing_acceptance` — the bead or governing spec
  is ambiguous, unverifiable, contradictory, or missing acceptance criteria. DDx
  blocks the bead with `needs_human` and does not ask another implementer to
  guess.
- `review_too_large` — the result or bead is too broad for bounded review. DDx
  runs the intake/decomposition path, blocks or decomposes the parent, and does
  not re-run the same monolithic implementation attempt.
- `review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope` — the implementation changed forbidden scope,
  removed checks, weakened behavior, or otherwise needs explicit repair. If the
  repair is mechanical it follows `review_fixable_gap`; otherwise it blocks with
  `needs_human`.

Review errors retry the review path up to `review_max_retries` for the same
`result_rev` and reviewer slot. On exhaustion, DDx emits
`review-manual-required`, blocks the bead with `needs_human`, and parks it for
operator review without closing it. A new implementation result starts a new
review-error retry scope.

Each implementation/review cycle is append-only. A repair attempt creates a new
cycle record linked to the prior review group (`repair_context_from_review_group`)
and records its own `base_rev`, `result_rev`, implementer run ids, verification
output, reviewer run ids, aggregate verdict, retry/decomposition/block decision,
and cost summary. No cycle overwrites prior evidence.

### Cost Accounting

Primary attempts and review attempts both report usage and cost into the same
run evidence stream. FEAT-014 defines the normalized cost signal fields.
FEAT-010 owns the queue-drain budget stop. Subscription-bundled and local
providers may be excluded from billed-cost caps by explicit cost-class metadata;
unknown cost class counts by default.

Reviewer cost is not special. When a review invocation reports billable cost,
that cost contributes to the same drain-level cap as implementation attempts.
If the cap is reached, DDx stops claiming more work and records an observable
budget stop instead of disguising the stop as model failure.

## Consequences

- FEAT-006 owns request construction: `MinPower` / `MaxPower`, passthrough
  envelope, role/correlation metadata, and actual power recording.
- FEAT-010 owns intake, retry scheduling, stop-condition evaluation,
  no-progress handling, adversarial review aggregation, review retry scopes, and
  budget stops.
- FEAT-014 owns normalized usage, cost, cost-class, and freshness semantics.
- FEAT-022 and TD-033 own review evidence assembly and no-tool reviewer mode.
- DDx routing lint should treat concrete route mutation in `run`, `try`, or
  `work` policy as a regression.

## Non-Goals

- Specifying Fizeau's internal routing, fallback, provider-health, or model
  scoring algorithm.
- Guaranteeing a different reviewer provider or model. DDx can request stronger
  review, dispatch independent reviewer slots, and record degradation, not force
  a route.

data.text: I’m editing the agent package first: adding the intake hook factory, the strong-floor resolver, and the prompt builder/parsing code so the CLI and server can share one implementation.
The first pass is in. I’m tightening one compile-time edge now: the intake prompt builder needs to read prior attempt events without assuming every bead reader exposes the event API.
I’m checking the upstream Fizeau service interface before I write the test stub. That keeps the test local and avoids a compile churn from missing methods.

I’ve got the target wiring and the new intake hook skeleton in place. Next I’m validating that hook against the existing agent/runtime contracts, then I’ll wire it into `ddx work` and the server worker path, add the acceptance tests, and run the required gates.

I’m reading the exact hook and runtime code now to make sure the wiring matches the existing execution flow rather than bolting on a parallel path. After that I’ll patch the call sites and put the tests directly against the runtime behavior.

package agent

import (
	"context"
	"go/parser"
	"go/token"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"strings"
	"sync/atomic"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type triageHookRunnerStub struct {
	lastOpts RunArgs
	calls    int32
	run      func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error)
}

func (r *triageHookRunnerStub) Run(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
	r.lastOpts = opts
	atomic.AddInt32(&r.calls, 1)
	if r.run != nil {
		return r.run(opts)
	}
	return &Result{ExitCode: 0, Output: `{"classification":"transport","recommended_action":"retry","rationale":"transient","suggested_amendments":"none","suggested_followup_beads":[]}`}, nil
}

func newTriageHookTestRoot(t *testing.T) string {
	t.Helper()
	root := t.TempDir()
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx"), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(root, ResolveLogDir(root, "")), 0o755))
	return root
}

func newTriageHookTestStore(t *testing.T, root string) (*bead.Store, *bead.Bead) {
	t.Helper()
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx"))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	b := &bead.Bead{
		ID:          "ddx-triage-1",
		Title:       "agent: invoke runner-backed post-attempt triage hook",
		IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
		Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:    2,
		Labels:      []string{"phase:2", "area:agent", "kind:feature"},
		Description: "PROBLEM\npost-attempt triage is missing\n\nROOT CAUSE\nexecute_bead_loop.go does not invoke a triage hook\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nadd a runner-backed hook\n\nNON-SCOPE\nreview triage\n",
		Acceptance:  "1. TestTriageHook_UsesRunnerLibrary\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n3. lefthook run pre-commit passes",
		Notes:       "triage hook fixture",
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
	return store, b
}

func triageHookTestConfig() config.ResolvedConfig {
	cfg := config.NewTestConfigForRun(config.TestRunConfigOpts{Harness: "codex"})
	return cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
}

func TestTriageHook_UsesRunnerLibrary(t *testing.T) {
	root := newTriageHookTestRoot(t)
	store, b := newTriageHookTestStore(t, root)
	require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
		Kind:      "bead-quality.lint",
		Summary:   "score=9",
		Body:      "first event",
		Source:    "test",
		CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
	}))

	runner := &triageHookRunnerStub{}
	hook := NewPostAttemptTriageHook(root, store, triageHookTestConfig(), nil, runner, nil)

	got, err := hook(context.Background(), b.ID, ExecuteBeadReport{
		BeadID:    b.ID,
		Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
		Detail:    "transport error",
		BaseRev:   "feedface",
		ResultRev: "feedface",
		SessionID: "sess-1",
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "transport", got.Classification)
	assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&runner.calls))

	assert.Equal(t, postAttemptTriagePromptSource, runner.lastOpts.PromptSource)
	assert.Equal(t, root, runner.lastOpts.WorkDir)
	assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(runner.lastOpts.Prompt, "MODE: triage"))
	assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, postAttemptTriageSkillName)
	assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, `"execute_bead_report":`)
	assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, `"classification"`)
	assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, `"recommended_action"`)
	assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, `"suggested_followup_beads"`)

	_, thisFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
	require.True(t, ok)
	triageHookPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(thisFile), "triage_hook.go")
	fset := token.NewFileSet()
	parsed, err := parser.ParseFile(fset, triageHookPath, nil, parser.ImportsOnly)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	for _, imp := range parsed.Imports {
		assert.NotEqual(t, `"os/exec"`, imp.Path.Value, "triage_hook.go must not import os/exec")
	}
	body, err := os.ReadFile(triageHookPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotContains(t, string(body), "ddx agent run", "triage_hook.go must not shell out to ddx agent run")
}

func TestTriageHook_PromptIncludesOutcomeAndLogExcerpt(t *testing.T) {

package agent

import (
	"context"
	"go/parser"
	"go/token"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type lintHookRunnerStub struct {
	lastOpts RunArgs
	run      func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error)
}

func (r *lintHookRunnerStub) Run(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
	r.lastOpts = opts
	if r.run != nil {
		return r.run(opts)
	}
	return &Result{ExitCode: 0}, nil
}

func newLintHookTestRoot(t *testing.T) string {
	t.Helper()

	root := t.TempDir()
	skillPath := filepath.Join(root, ".agents", "skills", "ddx", "bead-lifecycle")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillPath, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(skillPath, "SKILL.md"), []byte("lint"), 0o644))
	return root
}

func newLintHookTestStore(t *testing.T, root string) (*bead.Store, *bead.Bead) {
	t.Helper()

	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx"))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())

	dep := &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "ddx-lint-dep",
		Title:     "dependency bead for lint hook fixture",
		IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:  2,
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(dep))

	b := &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "ddx-lint-001",
		Title:     "agent: implement runner-backed bead lifecycle lint hook",
		IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:  2,
		Labels:    []string{"phase:2", "area:agent", "kind:feature"},
		Parent:    "ddx-parent",
		Dependencies: []bead.Dependency{
			{IssueID: "ddx-lint-001", DependsOnID: "ddx-lint-dep", Type: "blocks"},
		},
		Description: "PROBLEM\nmissing lint hook\n\nROOT CAUSE\nno hook\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nadd hook\n\nNON-SCOPE\ntriage\n",
		Acceptance:  "1. TestLintHook_UsesRunnerLibrary\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n3. lefthook run pre-commit passes",
		Notes:       "lint hook test fixture",
		Extra: map[string]any{
			"spec-id":            "FEAT-999",
			"execution-eligible": true,
		},
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
	return store, b
}

func lintHookTestConfig() config.ResolvedConfig {
	cfg := config.NewTestConfigForRun(config.TestRunConfigOpts{Harness: "codex"})
	return cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
}

func TestLintHook_UsesRunnerLibrary(t *testing.T) {
	root := newLintHookTestRoot(t)
	store, b := newLintHookTestStore(t, root)

	runner := &lintHookRunnerStub{}
	runner.run = func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
		return &Result{
			ExitCode: 0,
			Output:   `{"score":7,"rationale":"ok","suggested_fixes":["none"],"waivers_applied":[]}`,
		}, nil
	}

	hook := NewPreDispatchLintHook(root, store, lintHookTestConfig(), nil, runner)
	got, err := hook(context.Background(), b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 7, got.Score)

	assert.Equal(t, "bead-lifecycle-lint", runner.lastOpts.PromptSource)
	assert.Equal(t, root, runner.lastOpts.WorkDir)
	assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, "MODE: lint")
	assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, "bead-lifecycle")
	assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, `"spec-id": "FEAT-999"`)

	_, thisFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
	require.True(t, ok)
	lintHookPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(thisFile), "lint_hook.go")
	fset := token.NewFileSet()
	parsed, err := parser.ParseFile(fset, lintHookPath, nil, parser.ImportsOnly)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	for _, imp := range parsed.Imports {
		assert.NotEqual(t, `"os/exec"`, imp.Path.Value, "lint_hook.go must not import os/exec")
	}
	body, err := os.ReadFile(lintHookPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotContains(t, string(body), "ddx agent run", "lint_hook.go must not shell out to ddx agent run")
}

func TestLintHook_PromptIncludesStandaloneBead(t *testing.T) {
	root := newLintHookTestRoot(t)
	store, b := newLintHookTestStore(t, root)

	runner := &lintHookRunnerStub{}
	runner.run = func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
		return &Result{
			ExitCode: 0,
			Output:   `{"score":9,"rationale":"ready","suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}`,
		}, nil
	}

	hook := NewPreDispatchLintHook(root, store, lintHookTestConfig(), nil, runner)
	_, err := hook(context.Background(), b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

func TestLintHook_BadJSON_ReturnsInfrastructureError(t *testing.T) {
	root := newLintHookTestRoot(t)
	store, b := newLintHookTestStore(t, root)

	runner := &lintHookRunnerStub{}
	runner.run = func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
		return &Result{ExitCode: 0, Output: "not-json"}, nil
	}

	hook := NewPreDispatchLintHook(root, store, lintHookTestConfig(), nil, runner)
	_, err := hook(context.Background(), b.ID)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, ErrLintHookBadJSON)

	var lintErr *LintHookError
	require.ErrorAs(t, err, &lintErr)
	assert.Equal(t, LintHookErrorKindBadJSON, lintErr.Kind)
}

data.text: I’ve found one correctness gap in the new intake hook: its strong-floor resolver can accidentally accept a low `--min-power` as the “strong” floor. I’m fixing that while wiring the hook into the `work` and server worker paths, then I’ll add the behavior tests around prompt order and power preservation.

data.text: The wiring is in place. I’m adding the last two behavior tests now: one at the CLI layer to prove `ddx work` hits intake before the rest of the loop, and one in the server worker path to prove the same hook chain is active there too.

I’m cleaning up the new test scaffolding before running the suite. There’s one package-level helper collision and a couple of imports to trim so the test file compiles cleanly with the existing server test utilities.

I’m at the point where the code changes are in place and the remaining risk is mechanical: formatting and compile errors across the hook file and the new tests. I’m running `gofmt` on the touched files first, then I’ll run the focused test set and fix anything that shakes out.
The focused test run is in flight. If it fails, I’ll use the failure surface to tighten the hook contract rather than papering over the tests.

data.text: The first compile pass exposed three concrete mismatches with the actual upstream types: the server manager has no runner override field, the service request doesn’t carry `PromptSource`, and the model catalog uses a different shape than I assumed. I’m aligning the tests and wiring to the real Fizeau API now.

data.text: The failing trace is useful: the loop is reaching real execution immediately after intake, which means my test is assuming lint runs earlier than this code actually does. I’m checking the loop’s hook order directly, then I’ll realign the test to the actual contract rather than guessing the sequence.

The hook order is fine; the test fixture was missing a required precondition for the lint hook to actually run. I’m setting a harness on the work-worker invocations so lint doesn’t short-circuit on “missing harness,” which should let the tests exercise the full intake → lint → execute chain.

+func TestDDxWork_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
+
+	var mu sync.Mutex
+	modes := make([]string, 0, 4)
+	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
+	stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
+		mode := "execute"
+		switch {
+		case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: intake"):
+			mode = "intake"
+		case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: lint"):
+			mode = "lint"
+		case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: triage"):
+			mode = "triage"
+		}
+		mu.Lock()
+		modes = append(modes, mode)
+		mu.Unlock()
+
+		switch mode {
+		case "intake":
+			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
+			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"atomic\",\"confidence\":0.99,\"reasoning\":\"single-slice\"}"}`)}
+			close(ch)
+			return ch, nil
+		case "lint":
+			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
+			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"score\":9,\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"suggested_fixes\":[],\"waivers_applied\":[]}"}`)}
+			close(ch)
+			return ch, nil
+		case "triage":
+			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
+			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"success\",\"recommended_action\":\"none\",\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"suggested_amendments\":\"none\",\"suggested_followup_beads\":[]}"}`)}
+			close(ch)
+			return ch, nil
+		default:
+			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
+			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
+			close(ch)
+			return ch, nil
+		}
+	}
+
+	dir := setupWorkIntakeFixture(t)
+	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
+	_, _ = executeCommand(root, "work", "--once")
+
+	mu.Lock()
+	got := append([]string(nil), modes...)
+	mu.Unlock()
+	require.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(got), 1, "work must invoke the intake hook")
+	assert.Equal(t, "intake", got[0], "plain ddx work must run pre-claim intake before claim")
+	assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(got), 2, "work must continue past intake to later execution stages")
+}

data.text: The full repo test gate found a real regression in `ddx work`: a config-pinned harness/model is leaking into the service request. That wasn’t exercised by the focused bead tests, so I’m checking the existing `work` passthrough contract and will adjust the wiring to preserve the opaque path instead of forcing config-derived routing into the service request.

data.output:
capturedReq = req
			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
			close(ch)
			return ch, nil
		},
	})

	// Build a project dir with agent.harness and agent.model pins in config.
	dir := t.TempDir()
	t.Setenv("HOME", dir)
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	cfg := `version: "1.0"
library:
  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
  repository:
    url: "https://example.com/lib"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  harness: claude
  model: openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini
`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfg), 0o644))

	store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
		ID:    "ddx-opaque-regression-test",
		Title: "opaque passthrough regression fixture",
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	_, _ = executeCommand(root, "work", "--once")

	// ddx work must NOT inject config agent.harness / agent.model; both fields
	// must be empty in the Execute request when no CLI flags are provided.
	assert.Empty(t, capturedReq.Harness,
		"ddx work must not inject config agent.harness into Execute (ddx-c4231775)")
	assert.Empty(t, capturedReq.Model,
		"ddx work must not inject config agent.model into Execute (ddx-c4231775)")
}

data.text: I found the actual regression: the CLI `work` path was still resolving config-sourced harness/model pins before dispatch. The `OpaquePassthrough` flag exists specifically to suppress that fallback, so I’m threading it into the top-level work resolver now.

data.text: The hanging server tests point to the strong-floor resolver doing real service discovery in the default path. That makes the hook too heavy for the worker-manager tests and for normal `ddx work` startup. I’m making the resolver use the configured strong floor by default and only consult a provided service stub when one is explicitly injected.

data.text: The slow path is almost certainly post-attempt triage: it currently runs unconditionally, even in tests that never install the bead-lifecycle skill. I’m putting the same skill gate on triage that lint already uses, so the default server tests won’t burn a real model call when the lifecycle skill isn’t installed.

Implemented and committed as `565f0457` with subject `feat: wire strong-model intake decomposition into ddx work [ddx-f58797f1]`.

What changed:
